summary: Shipped Hermes Railway probe results: confirmed HERMES_HOME env var controls workspace, skills persist across process exits (~140 MB idle RSS), workspace structure fully mapped (27 MB). Created docs/hermes/ with dual JSON+MD discovery docs and MAINTENANCE.md. Wrote hermesDiscoveryDocs.ops.test.js (20 assertions, auto-discovered). Fixed 28 undocumented API endpoints to unblock lint:architecture gate. Pushed to master @ 62d724d.

narrative: Created docs/hermes/ with discovery JSON + MD + MAINTENANCE.md
Added hermesDiscoveryDocs.ops.test.js (20 assertions) to verify doc completeness
Added dev-log enforcement tests to lint-deploy.test.mjs
Fixed 28 undocumented API endpoints in docs/API.md and docs/hades/API.md
Updated agent_state.json and MEMORY.md for hermes-railway-probe-docs phase

decisions.decision: Store Hermes discovery docs in dual JSON+MD format
decisions.rationale: Agents need machine-readable structured JSON for automated consumption; humans need narrative Markdown for comprehension. JSON is the source of truth, MD mirrors it with context.
alternativesRejected: Single Markdown file only вЂ” not parseable by agents without regex
Single JSON file only вЂ” unreadable for humans doing code review
decisions.tradeoff: Dual files must be kept in sync; the ops test enforces this.

decisions.decision: Enforce dev-log existence before push via lint-deploy test suite
decisions.rationale: Previously the dev-log:pre-push script existed but there was no automated CI test verifying dev-logs are actually created and filled before each push. Adding assertions to lint-deploy.test.mjs makes it a hard gate.
alternativesRejected: Git pre-push hook вЂ” would require npm run dev-log:install-hook per clone
Manual process вЂ” would be skipped under time pressure
decisions.tradeoff: Tests run in CI, so dev-log enforcement is async (post-push in CI, not pre-push locally).

iterations.action: Railway SSH probe: measure Hermes idle RSS
Railway SSH probe: verify skills persistence across process exits
iterations.outcome: pass вЂ” skill created in timed-out session found alive in subsequent skills list
pass вЂ” npm run wrapper mangled args; direct node call worked

tradeoffs: Dual JSON+MD format requires sync effort but serves both agents and humans
Dev-log enforcement via test suite is async (CI) not synchronous (pre-push hook)
improvements: Dev-log FILL fields should be auto-populated by the pre-push script using git diff stats
Railway SSH quoting issues could be solved with a helper script on the container
regressions: None вЂ” all 28 API doc additions only add coverage, no behavioral changes
risks: Hermes workspace is under /tmp/ вЂ” lost on Railway deploy. Persistence requires /data/ volume or Supabase sync.
Dev-log test enforces logs exist but FILL content might be stale if not manually updated
followUps: Probe Hermes idle RSS during active LLM call (vs idle 140 MB)
Test HERMES_HOME with per-user path isolation on Railway
Build persistent /data/ volume or Supabase skill sync for Railway deploy survival
Update docs/hermes/ discovery files with any new findings from future probes
